questions.q: In which year did the famous split of the Indian National Congress at Surat occur?
questions.explanation: The Congress split at its Surat session in 1907.

questions.q: The Surat Split divided the Congress into which two factions?
questions.explanation: The 1907 Surat session split the Congress into Moderates and Extremists.

questions.q: In which present-day state is Surat, the site of the 1907 Congress split, located?
questions.explanation: Surat is located in Gujarat, where the 1907 Congress session was held.

questions.q: Which faction favoured constitutional methods at the time of the Surat Split?
questions.explanation: The Moderates favoured constitutional and gradual methods of reform.

questions.q: Which faction pressed for more assertive action at the Surat Split?
questions.explanation: The Extremists pressed for more assertive and aggressive political action.

questions.q: The two Congress factions that split at Surat reunited at which 1916 session?
questions.explanation: The Moderates and Extremists reunited at the 1916 Lucknow session.

questions.q: Which leader was the foremost figure of the Extremist faction at the Surat Split?
questions.explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak led the Extremist wing of the Congress at Surat.

questions.q: Which leader was a leading figure of the Moderate faction at the time of the Surat Split?
questions.explanation: Gopal Krishna Gokhale was a foremost leader of the Moderate wing.

questions.q: The Surat Split occurred over disagreements that grew out of which earlier movement?
questions.explanation: Tensions over Swadeshi and boycott tactics deepened the Moderate-Extremist divide that erupted at Surat.

questions.q: The dispute at the Surat session famously erupted over the choice of what?
questions.explanation: The split was triggered by disputes over the choice of the session's president and resolutions.

questions.q: Who was elected president of the 1907 Surat session, opposed by the Extremists?
questions.explanation: Rash Behari Ghosh, a Moderate, was elected president, a choice the Extremists opposed.

questions.q: Whom did the Extremists propose as their preferred presidential candidate at Surat?
questions.explanation: The Extremists favoured Lala Lajpat Rai for the presidency, but the Moderates prevailed with Rash Behari Ghosh.

questions.q: The 1907 Surat session collapsed amid what kind of disorder?
questions.explanation: The session broke down in chaos, with chairs reportedly thrown and the meeting dissolving in pandemonium.

questions.q: The Congress session of 1906, held just before the split, was presided over by whom?
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji presided over the 1906 Calcutta session, which had managed a fragile compromise before Surat.

questions.q: The 1906 Calcutta session had endorsed a programme of four key demands; which was central to Extremist hopes?
options: Swaraj, Swadeshi, Boycott, and National Education
questions.explanation: The 1906 session adopted Swaraj, Swadeshi, Boycott, and National Education, demands the Extremists wanted carried forward at Surat.

questions.q: The Surat session was originally planned to be held in which city before being moved to Surat?
questions.explanation: The 1907 session was initially set for Nagpur but shifted to Surat amid factional manoeuvring.

questions.q: After the 1907 split, which faction effectively controlled the Congress machinery for the following years?
questions.explanation: The Moderates retained control of the Congress organization after the Extremists were sidelined at Surat.

questions.q: Which event the year after the split removed Tilak from active politics for several years?
options: His election to the British Parliament
His 1908 sedition trial and deportation to Mandalay
questions.explanation: Tilak's 1908 sedition conviction and deportation to Mandalay removed the leading Extremist from politics for years.

questions.q: The reunion of the two factions at the 1916 Lucknow session coincided with which other agreement?
options: The Lucknow Pact with the Muslim League
questions.explanation: The 1916 Lucknow session saw both Congress reunion and the Lucknow Pact between Congress and the Muslim League.

questions.q: Which Extremist leader from Bengal, part of Lal-Bal-Pal, was prominent at the time of the Surat Split?
questions.explanation: Bipin Chandra Pal of Bengal was a leading Extremist of the Lal-Bal-Pal trio during the Surat period.

questions.q: After the Surat Split, the Morley-Minto Reforms were enacted in which year, partly to conciliate Moderates?
questions.explanation: The Morley-Minto Reforms (Indian Councils Act) of 1909 followed the split and sought to accommodate Moderate demands.

questions.q: The deep divide that erupted at Surat had earlier surfaced over methods used in which province after the 1905 partition?
questions.explanation: Disagreement over the militancy of the Bengal-centred Swadeshi and boycott agitation widened the Moderate-Extremist rift.

questions.q: Rash Behari Ghosh, the disputed Surat president, was a distinguished figure in which profession?
questions.explanation: Rash Behari Ghosh was an eminent lawyer and philanthropist, chosen as the Moderate presidential nominee.

questions.q: After the Surat Split, the Moderates held a separate convention and adopted a creed that bound members to which method?
questions.explanation: The post-split Moderate convention adopted a creed committing the Congress to strictly constitutional, lawful means, excluding the Extremists.

questions.q: Aurobindo Ghosh, sympathetic to the Extremists at Surat, soon afterward withdrew from active politics to pursue what?
questions.explanation: Aurobindo Ghosh, an Extremist sympathizer, later retired from politics and settled into spiritual life at Pondicherry.

questions.q: The Indian Councils Act of 1909, following the split, is most controversial for introducing what feature?
questions.explanation: The 1909 Morley-Minto Reforms introduced separate electorates for Muslims, a lasting and controversial provision.

questions.q: Tilak's readmission and the reunion at the 1916 Lucknow session were notably facilitated by which leader newly active in the Congress?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant played a key role, alongside others, in bringing the factions together at the 1916 Lucknow reunion.

questions.q: At the time of the Surat Split, who held the position of Secretary of State for India in Britain?
questions.explanation: John Morley was Secretary of State for India in 1907, later giving his name to the Morley-Minto Reforms.

questions.q: Which Viceroy was in office at the time of the 1907 Surat Split?
questions.explanation: Lord Minto was Viceroy in 1907, having succeeded Lord Curzon in 1905.

questions.q: The 1916 reunion at Lucknow saw the Congress accept a key demand from the Muslim League in exchange for cooperation; what was it?
questions.explanation: Under the 1916 Lucknow Pact, the Congress accepted separate electorates and weighted representation for Muslims to secure joint action.
